text: I feel bewildered, as if I have stumbled into a space both public and clinical, partly observed yet detached. The fluorescent lights reflect sharply in the glass and polished floors, and the presence of another person increases my sense of self-awareness—my movements become cautious, deliberate, almost performative. Am I being watched, or am I watching? The boundaries between observer and participant blur, and with each footstep, I am reminded of my own existence and vulnerability in this modern, echoing environment.
